strategic_objective: Clarify the nature and stability of the dominant destination port distribution difference between DDoS and BENIGN classes to assess representation sensitivity and potential sampling bias.
key_checks: Compare destination port distribution across multiple time frames and data partitions
Measure entropy and dominant port ratio stability within each class over time
Assess whether the dominant port in DDoS is consistent or varies with attack instances
success_criteria: Identification of consistent port dominance patterns supporting targeted attack vector hypothesis
Detection of significant variation suggesting sampling or temporal bias
Quantification of distribution stability to bound representation sensitivity
